mission.title: Install llm-d Wide Expert Parallelism with LeaderWorkerSet
mission.description: Deploy DeepSeek-R1-0528 using vLLM's P/D disaggregation with NIXL in a wide expert parallel pattern using LeaderWorkerSets (LWS). This configuration uses DP=16 for both prefill and decode workers across 32 GPUs. Validated on 32xH200 and 32xB200 clusters with InfiniBand or RoCE networking on GKE and CoreWeave.

steps.title: Install prerequisite client tools
steps.description:
Ensure you have:
- `helm` (v3+)
- `helmfile` (v0.169+)
- `kubectl` (v1.29+)
- `yq` (v4+)

See the [client setup guide](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/client-setup).

steps.title: Prepare cluster with RDMA and LeaderWorkerSet
steps.description:
This guide requires:
1. 32 NVIDIA H200 or B200 GPUs with InfiniBand or RoCE RDMA networking
2. Full mesh network connectivity (DeepEP requires All-to-All RDMA; rail-only connectivity will fail)
3. [LeaderWorkerSet controller v0.7.0+](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/infrastructure#optional-install-leaderworkerset-for-multi-host-inference) installed
4. Gateway API CRDs and a gateway provider
5. Monitoring stack

See [infrastructure prerequisites](https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/prereq/infrastructure).

steps.title: Create namespace and HuggingFace token secret
steps.description:
```bash
export NAMESPACE=llm-d-wide-ep
kubectl create namespace ${NAMESPACE}

kubectl create secret generic llm-d-hf-token \
  --from-literal=HF_TOKEN=<your-hf-token> \
  -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Deploy model servers with kustomize
steps.description:
Navigate to the guide directory and deploy for your platform:
```bash
cd guides/wide-ep-lws/
```

**GKE (H200):**
```bash
kubectl apply -k ./manifests/modelserver/gke -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**GKE (B200):**
```bash
kubectl apply -k ./manifests/modelserver/gke-a4 -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

**CoreWeave:**
```bash
kubectl apply -k ./manifests/modelserver/coreweave -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

steps.title: Deploy InferencePool
steps.description:
Install the InferencePool Helm chart for your gateway provider:

**GKE:**
```bash
helm install llm-d-infpool \
  -n ${NAMESPACE} \
  -f ./manifests/inferencepool.values.yaml \
  --set "provider.name=gke" \
  oci://registry.k8s.io/gateway-api-inference-extension/charts/inferencepool \
  --version v1.3.0
```

**Istio:**
```bash
helm install llm-d-infpool \
  -n ${NAMESPACE} \
  -f ./manifests/inferencepool.values.yaml \
  --set "provider.name=istio" \
  oci://registry.k8s.io/gateway-api-inference-extension/charts/inferencepool \
  --version v1.3.0
```

steps.title: Deploy Gateway and HTTPRoute
steps.description:
Deploy using the gateway recipe:
```bash
# See the gateway recipe guide for your provider
# https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/tree/main/guides/recipes/gateway
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check Helm releases:
```bash
helm list -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Expected: `llm-d-infpool` in deployed status.

Verify resources:
```bash
kubectl get all -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Expected: EPP deployment, gateway deployment, decode StatefulSets (leader + workers), prefill StatefulSets (leader + workers).

**Note:** vLLM API servers take 7-10 minutes to start up for this configuration.

resolution.summary: A successful deployment shows the InferencePool Helm release, gateway, EPP, and LeaderWorkerSet-managed decode and prefill pods all running. Each set uses DP=16 parallelism across 32 GPUs.

uninstall.title: Remove Gateway resources
uninstall.description:
```bash
kubectl delete -k ../recipes/gateway/<your-provider> -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

Replace `<your-provider>` with your gateway recipe directory (gke-l7-regional-external-managed, istio, kgateway, etc.).

upgrade.title: Update manifests to new version
upgrade.description: Pull the latest llm-d repository and update the kustomize manifests and InferencePool chart version.

troubleshooting.title: LeaderWorkerSet pods not scheduling
troubleshooting.description:
Verify the LWS controller is installed and running:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n lws-system
```

Ensure version v0.7.0+ is installed. Check that nodes have the required GPU resources and RDMA capabilities.

troubleshooting.title: RDMA connectivity failures between pods
troubleshooting.description:
DeepEP requires All-to-All RDMA connectivity. Rail-only connectivity (where NICs can only communicate with matching NIC IDs on other hosts) will fail. Verify full mesh network connectivity:
```bash
kubectl logs <decode-pod> -n ${NAMESPACE} --tail=100
```

troubleshooting.title: Long startup time
troubleshooting.description:
vLLM API servers take 7-10 minutes to start with this configuration due to the large model size and parallel initialization. Monitor startup progress:
```bash
kubectl logs -f <pod-name> -n ${NAMESPACE}
```

troubleshooting.title: Out of memory errors
troubleshooting.description: DeepSeek-R1-0528 is a very large model. Ensure all 32 GPUs are available with sufficient VRAM. Check pod resource requests match the available GPU memory on your nodes.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster with 32 NVIDIA H200 or B200 GPUs, InfiniBand or RoCE RDMA networking with full mesh connectivity, LeaderWorkerSet controller v0.7.0+, Gateway API CRDs, a gateway provider, monitoring stack, and a HuggingFace token.
